Now. in what is here written this
priest does not mean that the ten
dency is to grow worse; but he
means that with shorter hours and
inreased wages there is a tendency
to go wrong which must be offset
by movements such as this great
temperance movement and similar
efforts for social and civic better
ment. or else the increase in leis-
ure and money will prove a curse
instead of blessing. | strive never

perate, and hardworking and

The hand of the Lord is some
times heavy upon the just as well
as upon the unjust, and in the life
of labor and effort with We must
lead on this earth it is not always
possible either by work, by wisdom
or by upright behavior to ward off
disaster. But it is most emphati-
ally true that the nance for leading
happy and prosperous life is I'M
mensely improved if only the man
is decent, sober, Industrious and ex-
ercises foresight and judgment.
Let him remember above all that
the performance of duty is the first
essential to right living, and that
a good type of average family life
is the corner stone of national nap
piness and greatness. No man can
be a good citizen. can deserve the
respect of his fellows, unless first
of all he is a good man in his own
family, unless he does his duty
faithfully by his wife and children.
